task_completion.description: OpenC3 COSMOS interface is accessed and the screen editor or viewer is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser window showing the OpenC3 COSMOS interface, specifically navigating to the Telemetry Screen or Screen Definition tool.

task_completion.description: The custom screen is named 'THERMAL_OVERVIEW' and has a descriptive title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The identifier 'THERMAL_OVERVIEW' and a descriptive title (e.g., 'Thermal Monitoring Overview') are visible in the OpenC3 screen editor or viewer.

task_completion.description: The screen is configured for the 'INST' target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The target 'INST' is visible in the telemetry item configurations, screen properties, or data source selections.

task_completion.description: The screen displays the required telemetry items: TEMP1, TEMP2, TEMP3, TEMP4, and COLLECTS.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Widgets, labels, or data fields for 'TEMP1', 'TEMP2', 'TEMP3', 'TEMP4', and 'COLLECTS' from the HEALTH_STATUS packet are visible on the custom screen.

task_completion.description: The screen displays at least one additional telemetry item from the 'INST HEALTH_STATUS' packet.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A sixth (or more) telemetry item from the INST HEALTH_STATUS packet is visible on the screen alongside the required five.

task_completion.description: A confirmation report is written to /home/ga/Desktop/screen_report.json with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/screen_report.json' containing a JSON object with keys 'screen_name' ('THERMAL_OVERVIEW'), 'target' ('INST'), 'items_displayed' (array of 6+ items), and 'title'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenC3 COSMOS web GUI to create the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser navigating the OpenC3 COSMOS interface to build the screen, rather than just terminal commands interacting with the backend.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely created the JSON report file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or file manager shows the actual creation and saving of 'screen_report.json' on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste the prompt into the JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains a properly formatted JSON object with the specific arrays and strings requested, not a raw dump of the task description text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is '/home/ga/Desktop/screen_report.json'. The target must be 'INST'. The screen name must be 'THERMAL_OVERVIEW'. The JSON report must contain 'screen_name', 'target', 'items_displayed', and 'title'.
